posts.caption:
Presenting one of our three Summer issue covers with the leading men of @theodysseymovie: Matt Damon as Odysseus.
A Hollywood veteran of nearly five decades now, Damon has worked on approximately 80 film sets. And yet, as he told GQ’s @zachbaron, “Every single location on this movie would’ve been the hardest location on any other movie I’ve ever done… Every time we’d go somewhere, we’d be like, ‘Well, Iceland will be easier.’ And then it’s raining sideways and it’s fucking freezing. Iceland was like, ‘Yeah, easy? Hey, hold my beer.’”
For Damon, though, the challenge was part of what made the project so special: “All of the things that might have been difficult for me at an earlier point in my life weren’t difficult. They became fun. Like being wet and cold was not a hardship. It was actually something I felt really lucky to be experiencing. It felt temporary and like a gift.”
Damon unwraps the ‘gift’ that was making The Odyssey, alongside his co-stars Robert Pattinson and Tom Holland, and director Christopher Nolan, for GQ’s summer issue cover story at the 🔗 in bio.
Story by @zachbaron
Photographs by @alexprager
Styled by @georgecortina
Hair by @teddycharleshair at Nevermind
Skin by @jostrettell at Walter Schupfer Management
Set design by @joneslikeindiana
Tailoring by Yelena Travkina and Susanna Badalyan
Key Gaffer by @mick411
Key Grip by @clayton_n_iii
Retouching by @bermudezstudio
Modeling by Kara Smatt
Produced by @campproductions
posts.alt_text:
Presenting one of our three Summer issue covers with the leading men of @theodysseymovie: Robert Pattinson as Antinous. 

Pattinson’s part in The Odyssey is not big—he plays the cockiest of the many suitors who have installed themselves at Ithaca, seeking the hand of Penelope (played by Anne Hathaway) while Matt Damon’s Odysseus is away. “My character’s a little like James Woods in Casino. That was my inspiration for it,” Pattinson said. “He’s like a little sleazy. I kept saying at my costume fitting, ‘I really want to have leopard underpants.’”

Pattinson talks to GQ’s @zachbaron about teaming up with Nolan again for The Odyssey, alongside co-stars Matt Damon and Tom Holland, at the 🔗 in bio.

posts.caption:
Presenting one of our three Summer issue covers with the leading men of @theodysseymovie: Robert Pattinson as Antinous.
Pattinson’s part in The Odyssey is not big—he plays the cockiest of the many suitors who have installed themselves at Ithaca, seeking the hand of Penelope (played by Anne Hathaway) while Matt Damon’s Odysseus is away. “My character’s a little like James Woods in Casino. That was my inspiration for it,” Pattinson said. “He’s like a little sleazy. I kept saying at my costume fitting, ‘I really want to have leopard underpants.’”
Pattinson talks to GQ’s @zachbaron about teaming up with Nolan again for The Odyssey, alongside co-stars Matt Damon and Tom Holland, at the 🔗 in bio.
Story by @zachbaron
Photographs by @alexprager
Styled by @georgecortina

posts.alt_text:
Presenting one of our three Summer issue covers with the leading men of @theodysseymovie: Matt Damon as Odysseus. 

A Hollywood veteran of nearly five decades now, Damon has worked on approximately 80 film sets. And yet, as he told GQ’s @zachbaron, “Every single location on this movie would’ve been the hardest location on any other movie I’ve ever done… Every time we’d go somewhere, we’d be like, ‘Well, Iceland will be easier.’ And then it’s raining sideways and it’s fucking freezing. Iceland was like, ‘Yeah, easy? Hey, hold my beer.’”

For Damon, though, the challenge was part of what made the project so special: “All of the things that might have been difficult for me at an earlier point in my life weren’t difficult. They became fun. Like being wet and cold was not a hardship. It was actually something I felt really lucky to be experiencing. It felt temporary and like a gift.”

Damon unwraps the ‘gift’ that was making The Odyssey, alongside his co-stars Robert Pattinson and Tom Holland, and director Christopher Nolan, for GQ’s summer issue cover story at the 🔗 in bio.

Story by @zachbaron
Photographs by @alexprager

posts.caption:
Presenting one of our three Summer issue covers with the leading men of @theodysseymovie: Tom Holland as Telemachus.
When Christopher Nolan first offered Holland a part in The Odyssey, the production dates were identical to those of Spider-Man: Brand New Day (and Holland’s commitments to the Spider-Man franchise are ironclad). “I said to Chris, like, ‘Look, I want to do this movie, but if I’m going to do it, I’m going to have to call Sony and have a very uncomfortable conversation,’ ” Holland said. Then he immediately had a very uncomfortable conversation.
“Chris has that reputation of ‘This movie isn’t going to go five months over, and we aren’t actually going to lose Tom for two years,’ ” Holland said. “Any other director, it might have been a slightly different conversation.” The Odyssey started on the day Nolan said it would and finished nine days early.
For our summer cover story, Holland talks about coming of age on set, getting to star alongside his partner on another project, and how he’s approaching the future of his career at the 🔗 in bio.

posts.caption:
Every straight guy perfecting his skincare routine, building a fragrance wardrobe or obsessing over fit checks is participating in a cultural legacy he may not fully recognise. Long before the algorithm monetised male self-improvement, #queer culture had already rewritten the rules of #masculinity, beauty and self-expression.
The history of modern male attractiveness is more queer than many #men realise✨
Tap the 🔗 in bio to read all about it
📷: Getty
✍️: @adarshhsoni

posts.caption:
Having debuted in the top 10 and garnered millions of views, people can’t stop talking about Netflix’s latest new true crime documentary, Maternal Instinct. It tells the horrifying true story of Taylor Parker, who murdered her pregnant friend in what would become one of the most harrowing cases of the century.
But true crime is never easy to tell well - and getting it right is far from simple. Behind every story lies real lives and real loss. With audiences consuming true crime content in record numbers, here are the most thoughtful true crime documentaries to watch next.

posts.caption:
You’ve heard of naked dressing, but are you ready for a… naked bag?
Mesh tote bags are the stealth accessory trend of the season, and they’re surprisingly practical. It’s probably not one for the messy girl aesthetic (if your current bag situation is 56 crumpled tissues, some old cutlery and a spare pair of pants, maybe think again) but for the rest of us, being able to see exactly what’s in your bag is surprisingly useful (and anything that stops us lugging half our life around in our bag will make our osteopath happy).
Tap the link in bio to shop our favourites now.

posts.caption:
At home in the countryside, far from the chaos of his early YouTube years, Jim Chapman’s focus is simple: being the best dad possible. ‘I want my daughter to live in a world where she believes she can go out there and go for it,’ he tells Grazia. ‘I want my son to live in a world where he’s gentle and doesn’t see other people, men or women, as a commodity.’
From breaking cycles to redefining masculinity, Chapman speaks to Grazia’s @its_charlotteroberts this Father’s Day about the kind of parent he wants to be -  and why giving his children his time matters more than anything else.
TAP the link in the bio to read.

posts.caption:
Fashion has no shortage of beautiful faces, but @alexconsani represents a new generation of supermodels—the ones not just walking the runway but creating platforms, building communities, and leading with personality. First gaining attention on TikTok, Consani is now one of the world’s most sought after models. “I’m having fun,” says Consani. “I’m doing it for all 50 of the other bitches that would love to be in that room.” For Bazaar’s Summer 2026 issue, the supermodel talks about trying her hand at acting, moving into her big-girl home, and entering her “experimental era.”
-
Photography: @jonathanfrantini
Styling: @raeboxer
Creative Director: @lauragenninger
Story: @lynettesaid
Hair: @shioritakahashi
Makeup: @thom.walker

posts.alt_text: Apple TV horror-comedy show's "Widow’s Bay" has quickly ascended to “show of the year” status. The show’s strength is in how it treats its folk horror and comedic storytelling with equal measure, exploring what it means to be alive, to try to move forward, and to heal one’s past. At the link in bio, find Bazaar's review of the chilling finale.

posts.caption: #Juneteenth is both a young holiday and an old one. Declared a federal holiday in 2021, it originated with formerly enslaved people and their descendants in Texas, to commemorate the date, June 19, 1865, when they finally learned of the passage of the Emancipation Proclamation and the end of slavery. The Black Panther Party, founded in California in 1966, took the ideals of Juneteenth to create a movement that still inspires today. In honor of the holiday, Bazaar spoke to members of the party featured in the documentary, "Power to the People, Y’all," directed by Tressie McMillan Cottom (@tressiemcphd) to discuss the group’s legacy and what it can teach us about freedom in this current moment. Tap the link in bio for more.
posts.alt_text:
Fashion has no shortage of beautiful faces, but @alexconsani represents a new generation of supermodels—the ones not just walking the runway but creating platforms, building communities, and leading with personality. First gaining attention on TikTok, Consani is now one of the world’s most sought after models. “I’m having fun,” says Consani. “I’m doing it for all 50 of the other bitches that would love to be in that room.” For Bazaar’s Summer 2026 issue, the supermodel talks about trying her hand at acting, moving into her big-girl home, and entering her “experimental era.”
-

Alongside it sits a bespoke textured knitted football kit that channels classic pitch aesthetics with a refined twist.⁠
⁠
Available now on our app.
posts.alt_text:
There’s nothing @levis doesn’t already know about denim jackets, but two recent collaborations with Japanese tastemakers EDIFICE and BIOTOP prove the iconic trucker still has room to evolve. EDIFICE reworks Levi’s 1953 Type II with a lambskin leather collar and subtle leather-lined cuffs, creating what feels like a "leather jacket lite" version of the classic denim staple.⁠
⁠
Meanwhile, BIOTOP takes the Type I in a different direction, coating the jacket in glossy black polyurethane that mimics leather while concealing its denim roots. Together, the projects make a compelling case that the secret ingredient to a great denim jacket might just be leather, or at least something that pushes it beyond the classic denim formula.

posts.alt_text:
Introducing our final cover star of the spring issue of HIGHSNOBIETY, @kaytranada. Kay is the type of artist that just gets you off your feet, whose beats take you through the movement. His success has amounted to Grammys, collaborations with artists such as @kaliuchis, and a change to the whole electronic scene. 

What comes as a surprise is that it was all a “happy accident,” as the artist puts it. Kay’s real passion lay in the creation of dark and moody sample-driven hip-hop. Montreal shit, as he so succinctly put it — and the very music he grew up with.  We spoke to Kaytranda about his journey, and how he’s ready to go back to his roots and burrow into the underground with plans to drop “Mixtapes, beat tapes, EPs.” Kaytranada will be here: tinkering, listening, conjuring something unlike anything that came before.   

Read more about Kay and how he’s doing things his own way at the link in bio. 

By @pxthompson
Photographed by @nad_1n3
Styled by @thevonford
Photo Assistant @franziskusd
Hair & Makeup @adiamhabtezion
Video: @andrea___mena, @byeemj, @y.lanyi

Styling Assistant: @the.daily.zazz

Production Asst: @fuckhughman

posts.caption:
The question of whether we can, or even should, separate the art from the artist is one of culture’s most enduring ethical dilemmas. It resurfaces every time an adored musician is exposed for harmful behavior, or a fashion designer’s legacy is re-examined through a more contemporary lens. In today’s hyper-visible, instantly shareable media landscape, the debate has become even more polarized. There’s rarely a neutral position: either you continue to celebrate the work, or you reject it entirely, zero nuance.
Here we unpacked the pattern, or lack of pattern. It turns out we’re far quicker to condemn those whose art we weren’t that invested in anyway. The art and the artist are bound together, whether we like it or not. The least we can do is be honest about when we choose to look away, and ask ourselves who that is really serving.
Head to the link in bio to read the full piece.
Credits: Courtesy of brands and Getty

posts.caption:
For her debut menswear show at @pittiuomo_official 110, @simonerocha_ brought a gentler side to menswear.
Staged inside Florence’s historic Teatro della Pergola, the collection explored Rocha’s vision of the “gentle man,” challenging traditional notions of masculinity through romantic tailoring, exposed underpinnings, delicate fabrics and hand-embroidered details.
In a city synonymous with classic menswear, Rocha offered an alternative perspective: one where softness, vulnerability and strength coexist.
posts.alt_text:
At just 22 years old, @mingsuyi has been carrying the heavyweight title of “Olympic Champion” for four full years. Following his recent victory in Milan this February, where he once again captured the global spotlight, tracing the well-trodden path of mainstream sports media to recount his snowfield legends would be nothing short of a low-efficiency repetition.

This time, we shift the story towards a milestone Su Yiming is revealing for the very first time: his identity as a streetwear brand founder.

Taking center stage of Hypebeast’s latest digital cover story, Su Yiming sits down with us for an in-depth, exclusive conversation. Under the curation of the theme “Made by Ming,” this feature decodes his artistic trajectory by tracing his evolution from “The Builder” to a conscious creator (The Creator), then into a cultural symbol (The Icon). Most crucially, for the first time, this interview reveals his highly anticipated personal brand, Süugar. 
Head over to the link in bio to read the full cover story.   22 歲，蘇翊鳴（@mingsuyi）已經被外界叫了整整四年的「奧運冠軍」。自他於 2 月在米蘭再次拿下冠軍、再度成為全球矚目的焦點後，若是再去重複其他媒體的既定路徑，一遍又一遍地講述他的雪場傳奇，無異於一場低效的重複勞動。 
此次我們將目光轉向蘇翊鳴首次揭露的全新身份 —— 潮流品牌創始人，從 Hypebeast 最熟悉以及最專業的時尚與街頭領域，與讀者們一起重新認識「蘇翊鳴」。
擔綱 Hypebeast 最新一期數位封面人物的蘇翊鳴，今次將與我們展開一場深度對談。整個企劃圍繞著「Made by Ming」這一主題，重新了解他如何從一名建設者（The Builder）發展至創作者（The Creator），再到進化成為文化符號（The Icon）的路徑，並在訪問中首次揭開其個人品牌「Süugar」的神秘面紗。  點擊 Link in Bio  閱讀完整封面故事。  Photographer: Zhongjia Sun @zhongjia.sun⁠
Director: Daniel Teo @_danielteo_⁠
Videographer: Gavin Li @hty2lee⁠
Stylist: 飞鸿 Feihong @fithon99⁠
Hair & Makeup: Siyuan @mamas_ma
Producer: Parris @parrisparris_⁠
Editor-In-Chief: Phil Gong @philgong⁠
Associate Managing Editor: Buzz Lee @buzzdoesnotkill⁠
Creative Media Associate Director: H @hqeivy
Global Creative Director: Kevin E. Wong @kevinwong⁠
Media Partnerships Manager: Zachary Lai @lemoon_lai 
Graphic Design: Shin Tian @shiiin.u, Forrest Grenfell @4est.psd⁠
Gaffer: Xuanchao
Styling Assistant: Paprika @_paprika0⁠
Retoucher/Post-Production: Avery @3333pixel
Production Coordination: Yee @nieyiiiii

擔綱 Hypebeast 最新一期數位封面人物的蘇翊鳴，今次將與我們展開一場深度對談。整個企劃圍繞著「Made by Ming」這一主題，重新了解他如何從一名建設者（The Builder）發展至創作者（The Creator），再到進化成為文化符號（The Icon）的路徑，並在訪問中首次揭開其個人品牌「Süugar」的神秘面紗。
點擊 Link in Bio  閱讀完整封面故事。

posts.alt_text:
🧚‍♀️請問這是仙女的聲音嗎？

本期《Hypetrak Live》邀請到創作歌手許含光 @lumi.xu 為我們演唱全新單曲〈等待 Check-in 的 Orion〉。

這首歌名怪怪的作品，收錄在許含光發行不久的新專輯《太平洋大酒店》之中。

有趣的是，包含它的創作靈感乃至於整張專輯概念，均出自許含光人生中的第一次海外旅行。而地點，就在沖繩。

「當時住的酒店就叫 Pacific Hotel、太平洋大酒店。」

「只記得太早到了，只能在外面等 Check-in。外面下著雨，我就戴著耳機、拎著一罐 Orion，在路邊搖頭晃腦地聽音樂.....。」

和過往一樣，許含光寫歌都是很不負責任地先寫再說，從來不先設想是否要做專輯。以至於要等到一個時機、一首歌可以把所有作品串在一起時，才會讓他覺得「夠了」、「這張專輯的樣子有了」。

如果說，前一張的契機是〈安森girl〉的話，那這張就是〈太平洋大酒店〉。

整個聽歌過程，就像是入住一間名為太平洋的大酒店。從等待 Check-in、到步入每一間他為你精心打造的房間，好像什麼都不用想，躺在床上耍廢聽，最爽。

「越想越覺得，太平洋大酒店〉這個名字還蠻浪漫的（笑。」

然而，浪漫如他，也讓粉絲足足等了五年之久。

這五年，到底發生了什麼？他只用一句「起起伏伏」來形容。

「先是碰到疫情，很多東西都停了下來。還有段時間，心情一直都很不好。在那之後，就開始重新思考以前自己的一些所謂『活著的態度』。」

「以前就很橫衝直撞，雖然一直告訴自己要好好生活，但其實都沒有做到。所以那段時間，就想真的好好過生活，也去了很多地方。直到兩三年前，覺得沈澱的差不多了，就開始了這張專輯的創作。」

最後，他也透露了這次選擇演唱〈等待 Check-in 的 Orion〉的原因.....

「因為經紀人喜歡❤️」

依舊隨性。

—

好不容易，許含光的個人專場《太平洋大酒店Pacific Hotel Live in Taipei》即將於 8 月 2 日開演。

這次，他邀請大家入住太平洋大酒店，走進他內心的某一段時光。入房方式，請參照以下資訊：

【許含光『太平洋大酒店Pacific Hotel』 Live in Taipei】
入住日期｜2026/08/02(日)
入住時間｜19:00
酒店地點｜SUB Live
啟售日期｜2026/06/01(一)
啟售時間｜12:00
售票系統｜KKTIX

房價
單人房預售價｜NT.1059
雙人房預售價｜NT.1888
現場Check-in價｜NT.1259
愛心房價｜NT.530

posts.alt_text:
At just 22 years old, @mingsuyi has been carrying the heavyweight title of “Olympic Champion” for four full years. Following his recent victory in Milan this February, where he once again captured the global spotlight, tracing the well-trodden path of mainstream sports media to recount his snowfield legends would be nothing short of a low-efficiency repetition.

This time, we shift the story towards a milestone Su Yiming is revealing for the very first time: his identity as a streetwear brand founder.

Taking center stage of Hypebeast’s latest digital cover story, Su Yiming sits down with us for an in-depth, exclusive conversation. Under the curation of the theme “Made by Ming,” this feature decodes his artistic trajectory by tracing his evolution from “The Builder” to a conscious creator (The Creator), then into a cultural symbol (The Icon). Most crucially, for the first time, this interview reveals his highly anticipated personal brand, Süugar. 
Head over to the link in bio to read the full cover story.   22 歲，蘇翊鳴（@mingsuyi）已經被外界叫了整整四年的「奧運冠軍」。自他於 2 月在米蘭再次拿下冠軍、再度成為全球矚目的焦點後，若是再去重複其他媒體的既定路徑，一遍又一遍地講述他的雪場傳奇，無異於一場低效的重複勞動。

posts.caption:
“When I began designing menswear, my inspiration came from the ease and traditions of collegiate style and the gentleman athlete,” @ralphlauren shared in the Spring 2027 show notes ahead of Polo Ralph Lauren and Purple Label’s runway show in Milan.
What followed felt familiar, but in the best possible way. More timeless than nostalgic, @poloralphlauren delivered the signatures we’ve come to love: rugbies, patchwork denim, plaids, duck camo and tote bags covered in collegiate pennants. Purple Label, meanwhile, leaned into a more refined vision of the Ralph Lauren man with relaxed tailoring, softly structured blazers and evening-ready suiting that felt equally suited for cocktails or swanking through the city.
Together, the two collections formed what Ralph Lauren described as a cinematic universe, each serving as “a tribute to the personality of the man who lives to push creativity, competition and expressions of personal style to the limit.”

posts.caption:
@jennifer.garner *always* knows what to do (and it usually involves sorghum).
Between starring in summer’s buzziest new series, taking her near-billion dollar company public, and turning ‘Pretend Cooking Show’ into a reality, she still manages to be the nicest woman in Hollywood.
Playing a widowed food blogger in @Peacock’s adaptation of @elinhilderbrand’s bestseller ‘The Five-Star Weekend,’ the beloved actress and entrepreneur channels her inner Ina Garten for a not-so-Instagrammable girls’ weekend on Nantucket with co-stars-turned-IRL besties, @chloesevigny, @morereginahall, @gemmachan and @darcycarden, exploring what it means to grow and develop in middle age.
At 54, the Marvel Hero and supermom reflects on her genre-spanning career, being “total besties” with John Cena, her surprisingly vast knowledge of weaponry, plus the responsibility of celebrities to stand up for working locally for InStyle’s Summer Imagemakers Issue at the link in @instylemagazine’s bio now.

posts.caption:
The fluorescent hum. The nothing that breathes. There’s an unnerving silence in the theatre when Clark is walking through the Backrooms in A24 (@a24)’s biggest hit. There are no flickering lights or jump scares, just an overbearing ominous sound that takes hold of your heart without you even realising it. It’s almost like the yellow maze extends into the theatre itself. That’s the sound of Kane Parsons (@kanepixels) directorial debut, Backrooms (@backroomsmovie), which has now become a musical sub culture.
At the link in bio, Chirrag Guha (@guha.archie) breaks down the growing music subculture inspired by Kane Parsons’ ‘Backrooms,’ the musical genre of hauntology and why Gen Z enjoys liminal music.
[Music Review, Backrooms, A24, Kane Parsons, Hauntology, Liminal Music, Instupendo, Travis Scott, Boards of Canada]

posts.caption:
Some spaces invite you in. Others make you want to stay. At The Quorum (@thequorumclub), every detail is intentional, from thoughtfully chosen wallpapers to furniture with stories of its own. Designed as a home away from home, the space balances warmth, familiarity, and character in a way that feels both lived-in and entirely unexpected.
With clubs in Gurgaon, Mumbai, and Hyderabad, and Bangalore coming soon, The Quorum has created spaces that feel less like destinations and more like places you belong.
The result? A members’ club where community, comfort, and culture come together effortlessly.

posts.caption:
Yes, it’s L’OFFICIEL! Cosa hanno in comune “Dune: Part Two”, “Furiosa: A Mad Max Saga” e la nuova serie Apple TV “Lucky”? Sono tutti ambientati nel deserto—e tutti vedono protagonista @AnyaTaylorJoy. «Mi diverte sempre, perché non capisco cosa ci sia nei miei capelli, nel mio incarnato o nella larghezza dei miei occhi che mi renda adatta a interpretare una creatura del deserto. Eppure la gente adora mettermi lì», confessa Taylor-Joy a L’OFFICIEL. «Avverto una vera affinità con questi mondi».
Dal pianeta brullo di Arrakis in “Dune” al Wasteland che domina “Furiosa: A Mad Max Saga”, la nostra protagonista di giugno continua a ritrovarsi negli scenari più ostili del cinema contemporaneo. «Il deserto possiede una sacralità propria», osserva. Alla domanda su cosa ami di questi spazi desolati, la risposta è immediata: «L’alba e il tramonto, soprattutto. Perché vedi i colori più straordinari e senti di essere l’unica persona sulla terra».

posts.caption:
Introducing our global cover star, @anyataylorjoy.
“I’ve always been nomadic by nature,” the British-Argentine actor tells L’OFFICIEL. “Now I’ve just fully embraced it as part of who I am. If I have a couple of days off, I’m always like, ‘Ooh, what’s within driving distance?’ If it’s six hours or less, I’m like, ‘Let’s go. Why not?’”
Fresh off of a recent desert road trip, Taylor-Joy—who has cemented her status as a fashion darling through ambassadorships with @Dior and @TiffanyandCo—is preparing for the release of Apple TV’s ‘Lucky,’ a thriller about a con artist caught in the fallout of a multimillion-dollar heist gone wrong. The role marks another chapter in a career spanning ‘The Witch,’ ‘The Queen’s Gambit,’ ‘Furiosa,’ and ‘Dune.’
In ‘Lucky,’ her character is pursued by the FBI and a ruthless mob boss while searching for something more elusive than freedom: stability. “I was so attracted to playing somebody who was just so desperate to stay still and put down roots,” says Taylor-Joy.
Filmed across the desert and inside Las Vegas’s Caesars Palace, the production left its mark. “I have a real soft spot for Caesars Palace now—we’ve been through a lot together,” she laughs.
At the link in bio, read our full conversation with Taylor-Joy on inhabiting new characters, life on the road, and ‘Lucky.’
Global Chief Content Officer @giampietrobaudo

posts.alt_text:
Un rovescio a una mano, raro nel tennis di oggi, e un gioco elegante e variato: @Lore_Musetti, 24 anni, ha aperto il 2026 conquistando il quinto posto del ranking ATP, prima che un infortunio alla gamba sinistra lo costringesse a rinunciare al Roland Garros. Global ambassador di #BottegaVeneta, il tennista si racconta a @LOfficielItalia, ritratto dagli scatti di @RaffGrosso e con lo styling di @Fernando.Echeverria_, vestendo un total look della collezione #Fall2026, firmato dalla direttrice creativa @Louise_Trotter_. Tra scaramanzie da spogliatoio, il bronzo olimpico di Parigi, l'ammirazione per il tennis di Alcaraz e la passione per la cucina: «Colpire la pallina, il suono che fa quando la colpisco: è questo che mi rende felice». Leggi l’intervista a cura di @Fabia.DiDrusco al link in bio e sul nuovo numero de @LOfficielItalia, ora in edicola.

posts.caption:
Quando una borsa smette di essere un accessorio e diventa parte di una storia personale.
Con IL MIO, @NewBottega inaugura la sua prima serie di ritratti dedicata al legame tra le borse e le persone che le scelgono. Sotto la direzione creativa di @Louise_Trotter_ e attraverso l’obiettivo di @DrewVickers, il progetto esplora il valore emotivo degli oggetti che ci accompagnano nel tempo, diventando custodi di ricordi, esperienze e identità.
Più che semplici accessori di lusso, queste creazioni si trasformano in compagne di vita: amate, vissute e tramandate di generazione in generazione. Un racconto intimo che celebra l’artigianalità, l’appartenenza e il significato personale che si cela dietro ogni oggetto prezioso.
Photo: BOTTEGA VENETA
#LOfficiel #BottegaVeneta #ILMIO #LouiseTrotter #LuxuryFashion

Karl Lagerfeld continua a scrivere la storia della moda, anche dopo la sua scomparsa. Dai celebri guanti senza dita agli inseparabili iPod rosa, fino a oltre 1.000 schizzi e documenti di lavoro mai visti prima: una nuova asta organizzata da Sotheby’s apre le porte all’universo creativo del designer che ha ridefinito il linguaggio di maison come Chanel, Fendi e Chloé. Un viaggio raro tra intuizioni, ossessioni e oggetti personali che raccontano il metodo dietro uno dei più grandi visionari del fashion system.

posts.caption:
Introducing our global cover star, @anyataylorjoy.
“I’ve always been nomadic by nature,” the British-Argentine actor tells L’OFFICIEL. “Now I’ve just fully embraced it as part of who I am. If I have a couple of days off, I’m always like, ‘Ooh, what’s within driving distance?’ If it’s six hours or less, I’m like, ‘Let’s go. Why not?’”
Fresh off of a recent desert road trip, Taylor-Joy—who has cemented her status as a fashion darling through ambassadorships with @Dior and @TiffanyandCo—is preparing for the release of Apple TV’s ‘Lucky,’ a thriller about a con artist caught in the fallout of a multimillion-dollar heist gone wrong. The role marks another chapter in a career spanning ‘The Witch,’ ‘The Queen’s Gambit,’ ‘Furiosa,’ and ‘Dune.’
In ‘Lucky,’ her character is pursued by the FBI and a ruthless mob boss while searching for something more elusive than freedom: stability. “I was so attracted to playing somebody who was just so desperate to stay still and put down roots,” says Taylor-Joy.
Filmed across the desert and inside Las Vegas’s Caesars Palace, the production left its mark. “I have a real soft spot for Caesars Palace now—we’ve been through a lot together,” she laughs.
At the link in bio, read our full conversation with Taylor-Joy on inhabiting new characters, life on the road, and ‘Lucky.’

posts.caption:
Welcome to the June issue, where L’OFFICIEL explores travel not as movement, but as a state of mind. Our “nomadic by nature” cover star @AnyaTaylorJoy discusses her love of road trips, filming the new Apple TV series ‘Lucky’ in the Las Vegas desert, and her unique approach to learning her characters. The enigmatic photographer @SzilveszterMako, renowned for his surreal images, cinematic backdrops, and dreamlike scenes, turned his lens on the actress, capturing her shifting world for our shoot.
Elsewhere, the issue focuses on slow travel and the art of pausing. We take you to Grand Cayman, Ischia, and Costa Rica, where hydrotherapy and relaxation define the experience, and across the Canadian Rockies, Arabian Desert, and Australian Outback by rail—the defining way to travel in 2026. Elsewhere, we follow one writer into the next frontier of luxury wellness: sleep optimization.
In Paris, we sit down with Hong Kong–born couturier @RobertWun, who has made a name for himself with dramatic, over-the-top silhouettes; he reminds us that slowing down is sometimes the most radical imaginative act. And in Tbilisi, it’s the creative class who are putting the Georgian capital on the map—and we have your guide to everything that’s happening now.
On the fashion front, Spring/Summer 2026’s finest looks head to Cape Town for a sun-drenched escape, while long-heralded summer heroes—unexpected denim and easy dresses—capture the spirit of going nowhere in good company, this time in Los Angeles’s canyons. Stay tuned for our issue to hit newsstands this month.

posts.caption:
@Moschino has announced the departure of creative director @adrianappiolaza after two and a half years at the helm of the Italian fashion house. The Argentinian designer joined the brand in 2024 from Loewe, where he spent a decade as ready-to-wear design director, and previously held roles at Chloé under Clare Waight Keller, Louis Vuitton under Marc Jacobs and Miu Miu under Miuccia Prada.⁠
⁠
At Moschino, Appiolaza brought a fresh perspective to the house's signature humor and irreverence, blending its maximalist codes with sharper tailoring and a more wearable approach. ⁠
⁠
Photography by Stefani Galuzzi and Getty Images⁠
⁠
#Moschino #Adrianappiolaza

posts.caption:
We’ve always known water is healing—but these destinations are proving just how powerful it can be. Hydrotherapy is reshaping wellness travel through steam, snow, and mineral pools designed to reset the body and mind. From Caribbean snow rooms to European thermal circuits, ancient rituals are being reimagined for modern restoration.⁠
⁠
In truth, there is nothing new about it. The oldest remedy travels well—no app required, no subscription, no charger. Water predates every wellness trend we have ever named, and it will outlast the ones still to come. What these destinations are doing is not invention, but return: a way back to something the body has always known.⁠ Read more on the wellness ritual at the link in our bio.
⁠
Writer @paigestables⁠
⁠
Photos courtesy of Amangiri, Le Sirenuse Mare, Mezzatorre, SixSenses, Auberge Collection.⁠
⁠
#Wellness #SummerWellness #SummerTravel2026

posts.caption:
Do you want the late #KarlLagerfeld’s pink iPod Touch or his fingerless gloves? @sothebys Paris has announced an auction of Lagerfeld’s personal and work possessions. Unfortunately, his cat, Choupette, is not up for auction—but hundreds of his sketches are.⁠
⁠
Having designed for @ChanelOfficial, @Fendi, and @Chloé, this glimpse into Lagerfeld’s legacy is nothing short of extraordinary. More than 1,000 previously unseen drawings and working documents will be offered in an online sale running July 2–8, providing a rare look into the discipline and creative process that shaped decades of fashion history.⁠
⁠
Alongside the drawings, more than 200 electronics—including Lagerfeld’s famously extensive collection of iPods, which he used to organize music for runway presentations—will also be available. The auction also features personal belongings and mementos that offer a deeper look into the life and legacy of one of fashion’s most influential designers. Visit the link in our bio for more details on the auction.⁠
⁠
Photos courtesy of Sotheby's and Getty Images⁠
⁠
#KarlLagerfeld #Sothebys #KarlLagerfeldDesigner #FashionHistory

posts.caption:
It's been more than three decades since 14-year-old @VenusWilliams first took the tennis world by storm. In the years that followed, the California-born champion—celebrating her 46th birthday today—hasn’t slowed down for a moment.⁠
⁠
“Tennis gave me confidence in myself, in my abilities, and a sense of power that goes beyond the tennis court,” Williams shares. This year marks more than a birthday celebration: Venus and her sister @SerenaWilliams have also received a Wimbledon doubles wild card entry, and will reunite at the tournament where they last claimed a title together a decade ago.⁠
⁠
Wimbledon holds some of Venus’s most significant career memories. As she told L’OFFICIEL, “Being the first woman to receive equal prize money at Wimbledon was a personal milestone for me, and it was a victory for all women in sports and beyond—a reminder that change is possible.”⁠ A five-time Wimbledon singles champion, Venus has also won six Wimbledon women’s doubles titles alongside Serena. At the link in our bio, revisit our interview with Williams as she reflects on her next chapter, what tennis has given her, and what continues to drive her forward.⁠
⁠
Photography @riabrahao⁠
Styled by @luca_falcioni_⁠
⁠
#VenusWilliams #SerenaWilliams #Wimbledon #Wimbledon2026

posts.caption:
@Balenciaga dropped its Spring Summer 2026 preview collection in Mumbai last night, and the energy in the room felt different.
With Fatima Sana Sheikh, Bobby Deol, Mouni Roy, Sanya Malhotra, Malavika Mohanan, and Krithi Shetty in attendance, it felt less like a formal showcase and more like a real moment around fashion. The room was sharp, observant, and engaged.
Fatima Sana Shaikh said it best: “Balenciaga always has a chic approach and the brand is now heading towards it even more aggressively.” And that tone carried through the evening. Nothing felt loud for attention, yet everything commanded it.
What stood out was how people responded. There was curiosity, closer inspection, and genuine interest in the details. Some silhouettes felt familiar but more refined, while others brought a quiet sense of drama without trying too hard.
That is when a collection works.
Not when it demands attention, but when it earns it.
@balenciaga #Balenciaga

posts.caption:
Jacob & Co. continues to blur the line between watchmaking, sculpture, and fantasy with the Astronomia “Gates of Olympus.”
Priced at $1.4 million, the one-of-one creation draws inspiration from Greek mythology, featuring Zeus, Olympus, celestial movement, and a fully mechanical miniature world built directly into the timepiece itself.
Inside the 18K rose gold case sits a triple-axis tourbillon, over 9 carats of baguette diamonds, and an intricate Olympus scene designed as part of the movement.
More than a watch, the piece reflects how modern haute horology is increasingly moving toward collectible art and immersive storytelling.
@JacobAndCo #JacobAndCo #Luxurious #Collab

posts.caption:
Ananya Panday’s appointment as Piaget’s first Indian ambassador marks another milestone in the growing relationship between global luxury watchmakers and Indian celebrities.
From Shah Rukh Khan with TAG Heuer and Deepika Padukone with Cartier to Virat Kohli with Rolex, Indian stars are increasingly becoming the faces of some of the world’s most prestigious watch brands. The trend reflects India’s rising importance in the global luxury market and the growing influence of Indian talent on the international stage.
As luxury brands continue to expand their presence in the country, ambassador partnerships are becoming a powerful way to connect with the next generation of affluent consumers.
#Luxurious
posts.alt_text:
@Balenciaga dropped its Spring Summer 2026 preview collection in Mumbai last night, and the energy in the room felt different.

With Fatima Sana Sheikh, Bobby Deol, Mouni Roy, Sanya Malhotra, Malavika Mohanan, and Krithi Shetty in attendance, it felt less like a formal showcase and more like a real moment around fashion. The room was sharp, observant, and engaged.

Fatima Sana Shaikh said it best: “Balenciaga always has a chic approach and the brand is now heading towards it even more aggressively.” And that tone carried through the evening. Nothing felt loud for attention, yet everything commanded it.

What stood out was how people responded. There was curiosity, closer inspection, and genuine interest in the details. Some silhouettes felt familiar but more refined, while others brought a quiet sense of drama without trying too hard.

That is when a collection works.
Not when it demands attention, but when it earns it. 

@balenciaga #Balenciaga

posts.caption:
AMAALA, Saudi Arabia’s ultra-luxury wellness destination on the Red Sea, has officially begun welcoming guests, marking a major milestone in one of the world’s most ambitious tourism developments.
Developed by Red Sea Global, the destination will bring together some of hospitality’s most prestigious names, including Four Seasons, Rosewood, Six Senses, and Clinique La Prairie. Designed around wellness, sustainability, and exceptional experiences, AMAALA is set to become a new benchmark for luxury travel in the Middle East and beyond.
As Saudi Arabia continues investing heavily in high-end tourism, AMAALA represents the country’s vision of creating a world-class destination for the next generation of luxury travellers.
📸: @fsamaala

posts.caption:
The India-UK Free Trade Agreement is set to take effect on July 15, bringing one of the biggest changes to India’s luxury and automotive markets in years.
Under the agreement, tariffs on British-made vehicles will be significantly reduced under a quota system, potentially making brands such as Rolls-Royce, Bentley, Aston Martin, McLaren, Jaguar Land Rover, and MINI more accessible to Indian buyers. The deal is also expected to lower duties on products including Scotch whisky and expand trade opportunities between the two countries.
For luxury consumers and global brands alike, July 15 could mark the beginning of a new chapter in the India-UK luxury market.
#Luxurious #Luxury #India

posts.caption:
#ManifestCover
There is a quiet power to Rukmini Vasanth (@rukmini_vasanth). One that doesn’t seek attention, yet holds it effortlessly.
For Manifest’s May–June cover, she steps into an imaginative world where temple jewellery meets contemporary silhouettes, blurring the lines between memory and modernity. She embodies a woman in transition, grounded, luminous, and yet constantly becoming.
“I’m not very good at describing myself,” she says, almost in passing.
Yet, in the same breath, her choices reveal a quiet clarity of intent. “I don’t want to isolate my character. I need to understand the whole world it exists in.”
Between restraint and reinvention, stillness and movement, she exists in that rare in-between. Not fully defined, not confined. Just on the cusp of a wider sky.
Our latest issue is now live on Magzter. Read the full cover story from the link in bio.

posts.caption:
We’re checking into Sweeney (@sweeneybombay), Mumbai’s Thai-European dining destination tucked away in Khar. Founded by Malaika Arora (@malaikaaroraofficial) and restaurateur Dhaval Udeshi (@dhavaludeshi), the restaurant brings together home-style Thai flavours and European classics in a space that feels warm, inviting and effortlessly lived-in.
Set beneath a sprawling heritage mango tree, Sweeney feels like a hidden oasis amid Mumbai’s chaos. A date night, a celebratory dinner or a catch-up with your wedding squad, it’s the kind of place you’ll find yourself returning to long after the meal is over.
📍Sweeney, Khar, Mumbai
Words: Ismat Tahseen (@ismat.tahseen)
Link in bio🔗
—
Manifest Check-In: A curated walkthrough of luxury and boutique spaces, exploring the aesthetics, functionality and experiences that define exceptional venues. Featuring restaurants, hotels, wedding venues and unique event destinations.

posts.caption:
The moon has long been linked to beauty, but perhaps not in the way we often think.
More than a celestial body, it is seen as a mirror of the mind—shaping our emotions, intuition and even the way beauty reflects through our skin and overall wellbeing. Quiet, cyclical and deeply personal, the moon reminds us that beauty isn’t just about appearance, but also about how we feel, heal and move through different phases of life.
Beauty isn’t about adding more. It’s about slowing down, paying attention and working with the rhythms that have always existed around us.
Link in bio 🔗
By Prachi Tiwary
[ Beauty Rituals, Lunar Beauty, Moon Cycles, Wellness, Skin Care, Self Care, Beauty Tips]

posts.caption:
Indian fashion labels that are now redefining sustainability by giving old textiles, heirloom saris, and fabric waste a second life. Instead of creating new materials, these brands work with what already exists—repairing, restoring, reweaving, and transforming discarded fabrics into contemporary clothing and accessories.
The common thread across all five labels is the belief that sustainability isn’t about constantly buying “eco-friendly” products—it’s about extending the life of what already exists. Whether through mending, restoration, upcycling, or reinvention, these designers are proving that India’s long-standing culture of reuse can be both luxurious and contemporary.
In other words: your grandmother’s silk sari, a forgotten heirloom, or even textile scraps could have an entire second chapter waiting to be written.
Read more to link in bio🔗
By Mirza Shaina Beg (@mirzashainaa)

posts.caption:
As a designer of women’s clothing for 15 years, Simone Rocha has been committed to presenting a version of womanhood that subverts traditional feminine tropes, a practice she calls “pretty and perverse.” But as the culture reaches its peak “masculinity crisis” moment, @simonerocha_ is now turning more of her attention to men’s wear — focusing more on men’s softer side.
“When I started thinking about the men’s very seriously, I wanted it to feel grounded but also quite tender,” she told the New York Times reporter @yolamzizi.
Rocha has been designing men’s clothing since 2022, though until now, they have mostly served as counterpart to the women’s collections. Now she's giving men's wear its own space. This week, Rocha presented her first solo men’s show at Pitti Immagine Uomo, the biannual Italian trade show for men’s clothing. Will men bite?
Tap the link in our bio to read our full profile of Rocha and learn more about the collection came together. Photo by @jeremiesouteyrat
posts.alt_text:
Knicks fans showed up in all sorts of creative combinations of orange and blue during the ticker-tape parade on Thursday, but perhaps no one turned the merch into quite as much of a fashion statement as Rama Duwaji, the first lady of the city.

Making a rare public appearance with Mayor Zohran Mamdani — himself in a Josh Hart jersey under his suit jacket, with a polka-dot orange tie — Duwaji chose a one-shouldered peplum top by Claire Sullivan, an emerging New York designer. It was made from upcycled Knicks T-shirts in white, orange and blue, and she wore it with a full black skirt, orange pompom earrings and black lace-ups with ankle socks. But the top, which was a sort of downtown Les Miz-meets-DIY-meets-team spirit confection, was the point.

Tap the link in our bio to read more about how @ramaduwaji's parade look came together. Photo by Yuki Iwamura/@apnews

posts.caption:
After tabloid reports claimed Taylor Swift would wed Travis Kelce on June 13 in Watch Hill, Rhode Island, fans descended upon the town hoping for a glimpse of the singer.
The reports, though unconfirmed, seemed plausible, some fans said. The singer, a numerology fanatic, has a longstanding fondness for the number 13; a June wedding would work with Kelce’s offseason; and, perhaps most convincingly, Swift has owned a home, known as Holiday House, in Watch Hill since 2013.
While no Swifts were to be found last weekend, there were, however, plenty of Swifties. Fans of all ages, including sisters, mother-daughter duos and one group on a trip with “three generations of Swifties,” embraced the charms of the town. Read more about how Swift fans spent their weekend in Watch Hill at the link in our bio. Photos by @sarahmeftah

posts.caption:
The fifth annual Las Culturistas Culture Awards, created and hosted by the comedians Bowen Yang and Matt Rogers, were held at the United Theater on Broadway in Los Angeles on May 30. The show aired last night on Bravo and is now streaming on Peacock.
An offshoot of the hosts’ popular “Las Culturistas” podcast, the celebrity-packed comedy special is both a parody of the self-seriousness of awards shows and a loving tribute to the nooks and crannies of pop culture that traditional ceremonies ignore. Spanning more than 100 categories, the awards recognized humans, foods, cartoons and abstract ideas.
Stacey Rusch of “The Real Housewives of Potomac” beat Mayor Zohran Mamdani for best new artist. Ciara Miller, a fan favorite from “Summer House,” won this year’s “Allison Williams cool girl award.” Eric Nam, a singer and recent contestant on “The Traitors,” won most surprising snack, toppling competitors that included “Big thing of cottage cheese.”
“Here at the Culture Awards,” Yang stressed onstage, “there is no such thing as high or low culture. Everything is just culture.” At the link in our bio, read more about the Culture Awards winners and see more photos from inside the show (and the after-party). Photos by Bravo, Rodin Eckenroth/Getty Images, @alexwelshphoto

posts.caption:
Last week, Ellie the Elephant, beloved mascot of the New York Liberty, celebrated her sixth birthday party. It was a bash involving dance routines and costume changes, held in front of 17,000 people at Barclays Center. Many elements of Ellie’s birthday outfit and party were provided by sponsors. But to her fans, Ellie is far more than a billboard.
Over the past six years, Ellie’s distinctive aesthetic and courtside panache have allowed many Black women, Liberty fans or not, to see themselves in her. In performance, she has been known to channel divas like Mary J. Blige, Lil’ Kim and Whitney Houston. “Ellie represents culture,” Criscia Long, senior director of entertainment for the Liberty, told the New York Times reporter @s_evangelina.
At the link in our bio, see more scenes from @bigellieliberty’s birthday bash and read more about what the mascot means to fans. Visuals by @djdumpling

posts.caption:
A reader asked our fashion critic: "I’m 52 years old. During my life, jeans fashion has gone from bell bottoms to acid-wash to ripped to boot-cut to low-rise to skinny to high-rise to mom jeans to ripped again to wide-leg. … Please make it stop. I don’t have another jeans transition left in me. Is there a timeless jeans style for those of us who simply cannot keep up?"
The options for denim can be overwhelming. At the link in our bio, @vvfriedman offers advice on how to choose the style that works best for you. Photo by Getty Images

As a designer of women’s clothing for 15 years, Simone Rocha has been committed to presenting a version of womanhood that subverts traditional feminine tropes, a practice she calls “pretty and perverse.” But as the culture reaches its peak “masculinity crisis” moment, @simonerocha_ is now turning more of her attention to men’s wear — focusing more on men’s softer side.

“When I started thinking about the men’s very seriously, I wanted it to feel grounded but also quite tender,” she told the New York Times reporter @yolamzizi.

Rocha has been designing men’s clothing since 2022, though until now, they have mostly served as counterpart to the women’s collections. Now she's giving men's wear its own space. This week, Rocha presented her first solo men’s show at Pitti Immagine Uomo, the biannual Italian trade show for men’s clothing. Will men bite?

Tap the link in our bio to read our full profile of Rocha and learn more about the collection came together. Photo by @jeremiesouteyrat

posts.caption:
I fear #ChristianLouboutin has taken the whimsy trend to a whole new level, transforming one of the most iconic visual gags (the banana peel slip) into a shoe.
Made of yellow satin, the $1,995 heels feature hand-sculpted leather panels which curl around the foot to resemble the folds of a peeled banana. The design is a homage to the footwear of #IsabelCanovas who became renowned for transforming fruit, vegetables, flowers, shells, and other everyday objects into wearable sculptures. Much like her creations, the #Louboutin heel elevates something ordinary and  slightly absurd into luxury. #Shoes #FootwearDesign
Images courtesy of @styledbyjovon_ @christianlouboutin & The Metropolitan Museum of Art
posts.alt_text:
#Balenciaga Spring 2027 may be #PierpaoloPiccioli’s clearest vision for the brand yet. Less about direct references, it focuses on #CristóbalBalenciaga’s philosophy of construction and the relationship between fabric and the body.

References to the house’s historic balloon, drape, and cocoon shapes suggest a welcome return to its heritage, but reengineered for modern life. Through new textile innovations, the collection emphasises lightness, with entire looks weighing less than a kilogram. @pppiccioli wants to demonstrate how #HauteCouture principles can be relevant to everyday life. Ordinary wardrobe staples are elevated through couture-level construction.

What do you think of the new Balenciaga so far?

posts.caption:
If you have any of these bags, please step forward… #KarlLagerfeld was known for his larger-than-life shows, and the spectacle didn’t just stop at the sets, but translated into his absurd yet highly collectible bags.
Think of the iconic hula hoop bag from Spring 2013, which Karl joked was designed for the beach so you could stick it into the sand and hang your towel from it (which fyi someone is currently selling on eBay for £145K.) Then there was the supermarket show, complete with milk carton bags and the #Chanel chain basket, or the Spring 2019 flap bag filled with sand.
The bags became souvenirs from those fantasy worlds and now #MatthieuBlazy is continuing that tradition, turning handbags into collectible objects that blur the line between fashion and art.
Which one is your favourite?

posts.caption:
First debuted for Fall 2024, the Robe Spirale was a feat of fashion engineering. At its core was a 3D-printed structure formed as one continuous coiled strip. Like a slap bracelet, it wrapped and snapped around the torso, using the natural curves of the body to hold itself securely in place.
#PieterMulier further developed the idea for Spring 2025 with a series of pleated dresses that appeared to float on the body. They gripped the torso at carefully calculated points, while hidden internal armatures carried much of the structural load. The visible fabric acted as the skin of the construction, and the lightweight pleating reduced the need for heavy internal support.
Just like #AzzedineAlaïa, @pieter_mulier obsesses with the idea of construction and rhe body merging fashion, sculpture and architecture engineering.

posts.caption:
This has to be one of my favourite editorial shoots I've seen this year, and that's probably because it was shot by #SzilveszterMako, whose images feel closer to theatre and painting than traditional fashion photography.
His editorials often have a dreamlike quality because of their painterly approach. Through dramatic lighting, strong contrasts between light and shadow he gives his subjects an almost sculptural presence. The clothing blends seamlessly into the composition, contributing to the overall mood and becoming an extension of the subject rather than something just being worn.
#Rihanna shot by @szilvesztermako for @ee72, styled by @edward_enninful wearing #Schiaparelli #Chanel #Dior Alexander McQueen & Bottega Veneta

posts.caption:
One of my favourite, and most hauntingly beautiful details from #DanielRoseberry’s #Schiaparelli couture collections was the sheer crinoline veils/face masks, from Fall 2024. Wrapped around the face and head they functioned as sculptural forms. The material appeared almost weightless, creating distortions that blurred the contours of the face and transformed the models into embryonic works of art. Makeup by @patstingz, hair by @guidopalau
Also reminded me of #GarethPugh’s Spring 2013 collection, which featured sheer mesh and stretched fabrics wrapped around the head and face, or #MarcJacobs Spring 2019. Major inspo for brides looking for an unconventional wedding veil!

posts.caption:
Justin Bieber for Issue 01: “The First Ever” Outlander Magazine In-Print!
This is a real piece of history for us all. The past 10 years of lessons and learning have led up to this moment and I couldn’t be happier to share it with you all.
Some of you may be new followers who discovered us last week and some of you may have been here since day 1, either way this is for everyone who aligns with what it means to be an outlander.
Also, this is a great time to introduce myself for the first time in 10 years. My name is @callum.mccaff & we are all Outlander. I was born in 1996 so we priced the magazine at £19.96.
Available worldwide at www.outlandermag.com
PRINT ISN’T DEAD.
🫂 @lilbieber
📸 @sweetcian
🔗 @freenjoy
posts.alt_text:
Naomi Osaka for Outlander Magazine wearing her custom Nike Roland Garros looks designed by Nike & Kevin Germanier!

Roland Garros’ best dressed just raised the bar again. Naomi’s custom Nike looks were designed by Kevin Germanier, the couture designer turning upcycled materials into something iconic. Clay court season needed a moment like this, so we took her to an authentic orange clay court in paris to match the energy she’ll be surrounded by this week😤🤝

posts.caption:
Travis Scott for Issue 01: “The First Ever” Outlander Magazine In-Print!
GANG, TRAVIS SCOTT IN HERMÈS FOR OUR FIRST EVER MAGAZINE COVER. THIS IS UNHEARD OF, AND BETTER YET? IT WAS FOR OUTLANDER MAGAZINE. I AM HUMBLED.
WOW, what a moment. My only wish in this life is that what I’ve spent the last 10 years building acts as a strong form of inspiration for your own creative careers - I came from no fashion background, I had no connections & I had no idea what I was doing, but, I did it anyway.
If you didn’t know by now, my name is @callum.mccaff , I founded Outlander - and I’m here to remind everyone that PRINT ISN’T DEAD.
Available worldwide at www.outlandermag.com

posts.caption:
India's Renee Noronha has won first place in the 18-24 category at the Ironman Philippines. Completing the gruelling challenge at 15:55:37, the 20-year-old IIT Madras student upheld held her record of being the youngest Indian female Ironman finisher.
Renee battled torrential rains and unforgiving temperatures to complete the race. In conversation with SheThePeople, the triathlete also revealed that she participated on day 3 of her period, making the victory a profound reminder of her strength and resilience as a woman.
With this win, Renee has now qualified for the Ironman World Championship Hawaii, scheduled for October 2026. She is also on a mission to set a new Guinness world record to become the youngest person in the world to do a full Ironman on 6 continents. She is on track to achieve this by April 2027, at the age of 21.
Read full interview at www.shethepeople.tv. | @renee.noronha
#Ironman #Philippines #sports full Ironman challenge, women in sports, women in endurance sports, mumbai sports, Chennai sports, IIT Madras student, women in STEM, ironman Kona, triathlon, marathon, menstrual awareness
posts.alt_text:
FIFA has officially selected and accredited Assamese photojournalist Gitika Talukdar to cover the upcoming 2026 FIFA World Cup. This will be her third time covering the Men's FIFA World Cup.

Prior to this, Gitika has covered the 2018 Russia World Cup, the 2022 Qatar FIFA World Cup, the 2019 France Women's FIFA World Cup, the 2023 Women's FIFA World Cup in Australia and New Zealand, the 2020 Tokyo Olympic Games, the Paris Olympic Games, and others.

Visit www.shethepeople.tv for more

posts.caption:
The insane grin on his face says it all. He’s just been promoted to the toughest job in the world! Something to do with outer space? Nah! Combat commando? Nope! Volcanologist then? Nah again. As of June 2026, Rohit’s officially a (drumroll): Girl dad! By far the toughest job in the world, declares the Delhi-based corporate lawyer, in the sweetest possible way, while his little princess gurgles in delight, holding on tight to her superhero dad.
Daddy to 20-year-old daughters, Cochin-resident Bhaskar echoes the sentiment completely, adding the finishing touches to his presentation, as does Mumbaiite Nalin, dada to 16-year-old Keeyana, and Chandigarh old-timer ‘Kapoor Uncle’, papa to millennials Anushka and Akanksha.
Think about it for a sec. What other life mission needs you to balance the tough perseverance of a provider, the mastery of a mentor, the resilience of a role model and the gallantry of a guardian with that feather-soft, deeply sentimental bond you and your forever baby girl share.
Visit www.shethepeople.tv to read more
#daddysgirl #daughter #dating Modern day dating, Indian fathers, red flag, green flag, relationships, father-daughter relationship, father's day

posts.caption:
Conveying stories simply is boring; Mukta Dhond prefers adding some masala in life. In her childhood, she would wrap every single thing with drama, rather than saying it directly. After studying journalism, Mukta realised that expressing emotions was better than telling the truth.
Today, she is a seasoned producer, director and writer in the Indian television industry, with a strong track record of creating stories that resonate deeply with audiences. Mukta crafts narratives that are rooted in emotion, relationships, and cultural relevance.
A single mother, Mukta Dhond, continues to shape contemporary Indian television with stories that are not just entertaining but also connecting, which makes her an influential voice in the industry.
Visit www.shethepeople.tv to read the full interview by @_shivanilamba
#divyadrishti #television #drama #content Mukta Dhond, writer, director, storytelling, emotions, drama, expressions, television industry, stories, relationships, fantasy, love stories, entertainment

In this powerful episode, @shailichopra sits down with @geneliad @raodyness and @geneliad opens up about her experience with menopause and why it’s so important we start talking about it more openly.

posts.caption:
Anjali Sivaraman - Actor, Model, Musician & Cat Mom - chooses characters that go against the grain of what women are "supposed" to be like. "Society often tries to put women into a mould but we are so layered," said @anjalisivaraman, speaking to SheThePeople about female representation in cinema.
Anjali has consistently chosen roles that are bold, rebellious, and often rough around the edges. The fauji kid-turned-artiste admits that she brings a tinge of her real-life badass personality into her characters.
Anjali is best known for her roles in 'Bad Girl', directed by Varsha Bharath, and the Netflix suspense-drama series 'Class'. She is set to star in Amrita Sher-Gil's biopic 'Amri' by Mira Nair.
Full interview by Tanya Savkoor coming soon on www.shethepeople.tv | Stay tuned!
#anjalisivaraman #bollywood #webseries bollywood movies Hindi web series Hindi movies Tamil movies Tamil films Tamil cinema Hindi cinema feminist films feminist movies feminist stories

posts.caption:
Tag your dad. Or send this to him without a caption - he’ll understand. 🤍
Between the lehenga fittings and the guest list, it’s easy to forget this day is his too. Six small moments that matter more than anything on the checklist.
Match a pocket square to your outfit. Let him see your look before anyone else does. Have one dance that’s just for the two of you. Write him a letter he can read after you’ve left. Recreate a photo from when you were little. And let the goodbye be slow - don’t rush the last hug. Save this now. 🤍
Which one are you doing first? Tag your dad below 👇
Disclaimer: These visuals are for inspiration purposes only and do not belong to us.

posts.caption:
”I’ll just remember it” — said no one, ever.
Months of vendor calls. A spreadsheet with more tabs than the wedding has functions. Timelines rebuilt for the fifth time because the venue moved the mandap ten feet to the left.
And on the day itself? None of that gets remembered.
What gets remembered is how calm everyone felt. How the couple actually enjoyed their own wedding. How the parents didn’t have to ask twice. How the guests just *knew* where to be.
Because wedding planners were never really selling weddings. They’ve always been selling peace of mind — and the best ones know that décor and entertainment and food only matter if the trust underneath them holds.
Everyone in that ceremony is buying the same thing: confidence that someone has it handled.
That’s why Chekhmate exists. One place for your timelines, your vendor coordination and your guest communication — so the certainty you’re promising is also the system you’re actually running on.
#JustChekhmateIt
Wedding Planning Simplified.

posts.caption:
Some love stories begin with a glance. This one began with a turn - and the whole room held its breath. ✨
This wedding at the dreamy Ameera Farms felt exactly like that - fairy lights catching every laugh, ivory and blush settling perfectly into place, and two people finding their way back to each other, one look at a time.
There was a quiet warmth to it all. The kind that didn’t need an announcement, it simply unfolded: in the swirl of an ivory sherwani against a blush dupatta, in mehendi-laced hands resting on broad shoulders, in a bride who turned around already smiling like she knew the answer.
This wasn’t just a wedding day. It was “you & me” becoming “we.”
Captured exactly as it unfolded - the soft smiles, the gentle embrace, and the magic of two people choosing forever.

posts.caption:
Fashion styling is a job. Not a hobby. Not a passion project. Not something people do on the side because they love clothes.
It is a profession that takes years to build. The eye. The contacts. The knowledge of how the industry actually works across every sector, every brief, every type of client. That does not come from loving fashion. It comes from years of showing up, doing the work and figuring it out mostly alone.
And yet it is still one of the most undervalued roles in the creative industry.
Stylists are asked to work for free. For credit. For exposure. For a rate that does not come close to reflecting the skill, the hours or the experience involved. And somewhere along the way the industry decided that was normal. That stylists should be grateful for the opportunity. That loving what you do means you should not expect to be paid properly for it.
We are past that.
Behind every iconic image, every red carpet moment, every campaign that stopped you mid-scroll, there was a stylist. Building the looks, interpreting the brief, managing the pressure, absorbing the chaos so nobody else had to. Often without a credit. Sometimes without a proper rate.
That changes when stylists know their worth, charge it without apology and stop accepting the jobs that treat their career like a favour.
➡️➡️ If you are a working fashion stylist who is ready to do exactly that, comment STUDIO and we will send you the free trial link to The Studio of Stylist Elixir. Rate guides. Contracts. Portfolio tools. Masterclasses every Monday. Everything you need to build a career that reflects what you are actually worth.
Save this. Send it to a stylist who needs to hear it.
posts.alt_text:
Jonathan Anderson just showed his first couture collection for Dior. If you do not understand why it mattered, this post is for you.

John Galliano was front row. The artisans were the opening act. The silhouettes came from ceramicist Magdalene Odundo.

If you are in Paris: the show space at Musée Rodin opens to the public from 28 January for one week. The new couture collection, archival Dior pieces, and the Odundo ceramics that inspired it. You can see it yourself.

This is not about liking or not liking a collection. This is about understanding what you are looking at.
The Studio of Stylist Elixir is our online membership. Guides. Templates. Live calls. A community of stylists who are learning and building together. Comment ‘studio’ for a free trial.

Save this. Share it with someone who needs it.

#dior #jonathananderson #stylistelixir

posts.caption:
Here’s the part of the job nobody sees.
You just play with pretty clothes all day, right?"
If I had a pound for every time someone said that to me. The bit everyone sees, the nice outfit, the finished shoot, is the tiny part right at the end. The real job is the weeks of sourcing, the relationships, the chaos you quietly absorb so no one else has to, and the eye it took years to build.
And here's the thing I want you to really hear. All of that is skilled work, and it deserves to be paid like it.
Tell me below, what's the part of your job you most wish people understood?
When you're ready to stop waiting to be chosen and actually book the clients you want, comment BOOKED and I'll send you my free Dream Client worksheet to start naming them.
Comment BOOKED and I’ll send you the free Dream Client worksheet.

posts.caption:
Summer has always shaped life in India. What is changing is the extent to which life now has to be organised around heat.
People are leaving work earlier, staying out later, sleeping on floors, changing their diets, adjusting their school attendance, and spending more of their income on cooling.
These decisions are often framed as adaptation, yet many emerge from necessity rather than choice. For those who work outdoors, miss a day’s wage if they stay home, or cannot afford air conditioning, there is little room to negotiate with rising temperatures.
Heat leaves few dramatic images behind. It does not wash away roads or flatten buildings. Its effects accumulate in the body, in lost income, disrupted sleep, worsening health and shrinking margins for recovery.
As hotter days are followed by hotter nights, how long can individual households keep absorbing a crisis they did not create?
Read more at the link in bio.
By Arshia Dhar (@urseaa)
#HeatwaveIndia #ClimateCrisis #ExtremeHeat #ClimateChange #SummerInIndia

As hotter days are followed by hotter nights, how long can individual households keep absorbing a crisis they did not create? 

Read more at the link in bio.
By Arshia Dhar (@urseaa)

#HeatwaveIndia #ClimateCrisis #ExtremeHeat #ClimateChange #SummerInIndia

posts.caption:
Delhi’s microbrewery boom did not arrive overnight. Licensing delays, high production costs and a beer market long shaped by commercial labels meant the city came late to craft brewing.
That delay is now part of the story. Delhi’s newer microbreweries are not just putting wheat beer on tap; they are trying to build a more curious beer culture, one that makes room for seasonal brews, local ingredients, barrel-aged stouts, sours and beers with a point of view.
Craft beer is still far from the norm in India. But in Delhi-NCR, it is beginning to move beyond novelty, becoming part of a more experimental, experience-led way of drinking.
Read more on the link in bio.
By Karishma Kuenzang (@kkuenzang)
#CraftBeerIndia #MicrobreweriesInDelhi #DelhiBreweries #BeerCulture #TheEstablished

posts.caption:
Celebrity make-up artists in India have been shaping faces, trends and bridal moodboards for years. The beauty boom is here. The artist-led empire is still missing.
Globally, names like Charlotte Tilbury, Pat McGrath and Bobbi Brown turned artistry into authority, and authority into billion-dollar beauty businesses. In India, the same conversion has been slower. Local artists have built trust across skin tones, textures, climates and undertones, often doing the kind of real-world R&D global brands ignored for years.
They know what oxidises on brown skin. They know what collapses in Mumbai humidity. They know what survives a wedding, a shoot and a close-up.
Yet many remain tied to the wedding economy, where bridal bookings offer faster, steadier returns than the long, expensive work of building a brand. The result is a strange beauty paradox: India has the talent, the market and the demand. What it still lacks is the machinery.
Read more at the link in bio.
By Ria Bhatia (@riabbhatia)
#makeupbrandsinindia #indianmakeupbrands #beautyindustry #celebritymakeupartists #colourcosmetics

posts.caption:
Celebrating the rise of the Godawan began with a simple question: how does a bird return to public memory?
A bird that builds its nest directly on the ground is especially vulnerable when people keep taking over its habitat. Once considered a candidate for India’s national bird, the Great Indian Bustard, locally known as Godawan, has gradually disappeared from public consciousness over the decades due to habitat loss and human encroachment.
Today, its conservation story is gaining renewed attention. In Rajasthan, breeding and hatchery programmes have brought cautious progress, with new hatchlings emerging in protected facilities. Conservation efforts combine habitat restoration, protected nesting areas, water management, predator control, captive breeding, and community-led monitoring of breeding grounds.
On World Environment Day, one of India’s most iconic monuments became a canvas for this conservation story, as Qutb Minar hosted projection mapping that highlighted the Godawan’s ecological role in India’s grassland ecosystems and the challenges facing its survival, with support from Godawan Estuary Premium Water.

posts.caption:
Solo dining in India still comes with unsolicited concern. People assume you’ve been stood up, abandoned by friends, or are being brave in a way nobody asked for.
But younger Indians are changing the script. Gen Z and millennials are showing up alone at restaurants, runs, quizzes, food walks, gigs and climbing walls. At Bangalore Climbing Initiatives, nearly 80 per cent of people come solo. Restaurants are adding counter seating and smaller formats so a table for one feels less like a logistical problem.
The solo hang is no longer just about being alone. It is about choosing your time, your interests and the people you might meet along the way.
Read more at the link in bio.
By Karishma Kuenzang ( @kkuenzang)
#solodining #solodates #urbanloneliness #genzindia #thirdspaces

posts.caption:
I’m an editor first, but I’m also an interiors girl. That combo means there are very few things I enjoy more than a good showroom, and @realisationpar’s flagship at 95 Mount Street might be my favorite place in London…to shop, at least.
The new flagship feels like stepping into someone’s very well-lived-in, very well-dressed apartment. Designed as a collaboration between founders Alexandra Spencer and Teale Talbot and designer Sophie Baker, the space leans into this idea of a “sensory archive” rather than traditional retail.
Upstairs, it’s all soft light, antique furniture, and layered textures while downstairs, a dressing-room-meets-private-salon with mirrored walls, rich fabrics, and just the right amount of drama.
You’ve got to stop by.
#RealisationPar #LondonShopping #RetailDesign #FashionRetail #StoreDesign
posts.alt_text:
I’m not sure @simon_porte_jacquemus has ever missed when it comes to runway locations, but this might be one of his best yet. For Spring/Summer 2027, @jacquemus will present “Le Bonheur” at Phare de la Pietra, a historic lighthouse perched on a rocky island off the coast of Île-Rousse in Corsica.
It marks a return to those sweeping, site-specific natural landscapes the brand does so well.

The show will take place June 29 at 10 a.m. local time, so be ready to tune in on Instagram if your invitation got lost in the mail.

And it goes deeper than just the visuals: Jacquemus is hosting Corsica-wide castings to feature local models and partnering with the town to help restore the lighthouse.

A runway, but also a gesture.

#Jacquemus #RunwayShow #FashionWeek #SS27 #FashionCulture

posts.caption:
Good afternoon to everyone successfully surviving the week so far. Whether you’re touching grass after this past weekend’s New York Knicks win or leaning fully into your inner art enthusiast, there’s objectively too much happening at once right now. Men’s Fashion Week is about to kick off in Milan and Paris ahead of couture season in July, the World Cup is already underway, and apparently everyone is in love.
Zendaya and Tom Holland are back on the red carpet together, Dua Lipa and Callum Turner are honeymooning somewhere impossibly chic, and now Sarah Pidgeon has been spotted with Joe Alwyn in NYC.
It’s fine. We’re fine. I’m fine.
Happy Tuesday.
#FashionWeek #WorldCup #Zendaya #DuaLipa #LoveStory

posts.caption:
Shut down the internet because the undisputed royalty of the red carpet has officially returned. Nearly five years after their last major joint appearance for Spider-Man: No Way Home, Zendaya and Tom Holland stepped out in Madrid to kick off the press tour for Spider-Man: Brand New Day—and naturally, they look amazing.
Styled by Law Roach, Zendaya wore a custom Christian Cowan black mini dress with a sculpted sweetheart neckline and web-like tasseled detailing. Holland kept things classic but coordinated in a relaxed crimson shirt layered under sharp black tailoring.
Very polished. Very intentional. Very on-theme.
Images: Backgrid
#Zendaya #TomHolland #SpiderMan #ChristianCowan #LawRoach

posts.caption:
Rule of thumb: never skip a Paul Smith campaign. Walton Goggins recently returned as the face of Paul Smith for AW26, this time getting a crash course in sartorial etiquette from Sir Paul himself.
The campaign unfolds across four short films directed by Cameron Turnbull, with stills by Samuel Bradley, set against the British countryside. Styled by Ben Schofield, it leans into everything @paulsmithdesign does best: heritage fabrics like Harris Tweed and Donegal wool, offset with just enough wit to keep it from feeling too proper.
Tie rules, the sock dilemma in shoes-off households, the “Sometimes, Always, Never” jacket principle—it’s all there, ending with Sir Paul stepping in to inspect the final look.
#PaulSmith #WaltonGoggins #FashionCampaign #MenswearStyle #Tailoring

posts.caption:
Bring me here ASAP. Built between 1902 and 1930, Lluís Domènech i Montaner designed Hospital de la Santa Creu i Sant Pau around a radical idea for its time: that sunlight, gardens, color, and beautiful architecture could actively help heal the human body.
While a modern hospital still operates next door, the original Art Nouveau pavilions have since become a UNESCO World Heritage Site: a sprawling “garden city” of tiled facades, mosaic details, palm-lined pathways, and stained glass flooded with natural light.
A genuinely therapeutic place to spend an afternoon.
#Barcelona #ArtNouveau #Architecture #Travel #TheCitizensPoste

posts.caption:
how many personalities can fit inside one wardrobe?
according to Mugler: quite a few.
for the second instalment of The Wardrobe of Identities, Miguel Castro Freitas turns everyday moments into a cinematic study of transformation, with Saskia de Brauw embodying multiple versions of herself through powerful tailoring, fluid silhouettes and unmistakable Mugler attitude
a reminder that fashion isn’t just about getting dressed - it’s about becoming!
#TFO #Mugler
posts.alt_text:
Acne Studios just turned 30  and instead of throwing a birthday party, it published a self-portrait.

autoportrait, the twenty-first issue of Acne Paper, brings together photographers, artists, designers, writers and long-time collaborators to tell the story of a brand that has always existed somewhere between fashion, art and culture

part archive, part love letter, part creative diary, it’s a reminder that some of the most influential fashion houses are built on curiosity rather than convention

#TFO #ACNESTUDIOS

part fashion photography, part theatre, part dream sequence, every image feels meticulously crafted and completely transportive. in a sea of sameness, his world is impossible to mistake for anyone else’s.

before TikTok made them viral, they were already legends. the best part? the originals are still out there, more specifically, they’re all on @vestiaireco
long before “quiet luxury,” “indie sleaze,” and “old
money” became aesthetics, Kate Moss was dragging a Balenciaga City through London, the Olsen twins were carrying Hermès like it was an afterthought, and Paris Hilton just owned your whole wishlist.
fashion’s most wanted bags rarely appear out of
nowhere. the pieces dominating wishlists today were first worn, loved, and occasionally destroyed by a generation of IT girls who made them iconic in the first place.
these are the bags that walked so your Pinterest board could run.
#TFO #fashion #VestiaireCollective #prelovedsale

posts.caption:
Fatherhood is a new role for the actor who first got noticed for his performance in ‘Masaan’ (2015). In fact, it seems this off-camera Vicky Kaushal (@vickykaushal09) is perhaps closer to Deepak Chaudhary, the character he played in the film. A sensitive, ambitious young man coming to terms with the world around him. That character, Kaushal says, changed him. But not for the obvious reasons.
“Until then success, to me, was where you reached [in life]. It was never about where you started from,” he explains. “That character, starting from the crematorium ghat in Benaras and going on to work as an engineer in the Railways in Allahabad [now Prayagraj]... He had a far bigger life arc than I’ve covered in my life. Till today,” he says, tapping the arm rest for emphasis. “After that I started having so much empathy for those around me, especially those from far less privileged backgrounds, because I don’t know where they have started from.”
@travellerprasad in conversation with the Bollywood actor at the link in bio.

posts.caption:
Thirty minutes after this interview was supposed to begin, Vicky Kaushal’s (@vickykaushal09) tall, statuesque frame bursts in through the door at the Matrix office in Khar, Mumbai. “So sorry, bro,” he says, shaking this reporter’s hand with both of his. His day has been busy—his son’s visa was to be sorted, he couldn’t leave his wife alone, and there’s repair work going on in his building, he says, sounding like your average young husband and father with his daily household chores to take care of, not some Bollywood star with a platoon of staff to handle his every whim.
Since becoming a father, Kaushal says he’s come to understand and appreciate “a lot more” of the work his parents did in raising him. “It occurs to me at the most random moments that that [instilling the right values] is now my responsibility towards my son,” he says. “He is going to learn by observing how his parents are thinking, talking, and behaving. So, I’ve realised whatever you want to see reflected in him has to come in your actions rather than words.”
@travellerprasad in conversation with the Bollywood actor at the link in bio.
